data.title: Endeavour Silver Finalizes US$40 Million Sale of Bolañitos Mine to Guanajuato Silver
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Endeavour Silver ( (TSE:EDR) ).

On January 15, 2026, Endeavour Silver Corp. completed the sale of its Bolañitos silver and gold mine in Guanajuato, Mexico, to Guanajuato Silver Company Ltd. under a share purchase agreement originally announced in November 2025. Endeavour received US$40 million in upfront consideration, comprising US$30 million in cash and US$10 million in Guanajuato Silver shares, and secured two additional contingent payments of US$5 million each, tied to future silver-equivalent production milestones at the mine. The deal also gives Endeavour participation rights and imposes voting and lock-up conditions on its new equity stake in Guanajuato Silver, while capping its ownership at 9.9%. The divestiture allows Endeavour to redeploy capital and management attention toward its core silver assets and growth pipeline, reinforcing its strategic focus while maintaining financial and strategic exposure to the Bolañitos operation through contingent payments and shareholdings.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:EDR) stock is a Hold with a C$16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Endeavour Silver stock, see the TSE:EDR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:EDR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:EDR is a Neutral.

Endeavour Silver’s overall stock score reflects strong technical indicators and positive corporate events, offset by financial performance challenges and a difficult valuation environment. The company’s strategic initiatives and production growth are promising, but profitability and cash flow issues remain significant concerns.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:EDR stock, click here.

More about Endeavour Silver

Endeavour Silver Corp. is a mid-tier precious metals producer focused on silver, with three operating mines in Mexico and Peru and a pipeline of exploration projects across Mexico, Chile and the United States. The company emphasizes discovery, development and responsible mining as it pursues organic growth and aims to transition into a leading senior silver producer.

data.title: Pineapple Financial Delays Quarterly SEC Filing
data.description:
Pineapple Financial ( (PAPL) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pineapple Financial Inc. has notified investors that it will be late in filing its Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the quarter ended November 30, 2025, via a Form 12b-25 submission to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The company says the delay relates specifically to this quarterly filing and not to any prior reporting obligations.

The primary reason for the delay is the need for additional time to finalize Pineapple Financial’s financial statements and to allow its independent public accounting firm to complete its audit work. Management indicates that filing the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) on time would have required unreasonable effort and expense, suggesting the holdup is mainly tied to the completion of the audit process rather than to a specific disclosed internal control breakdown, governance issue, or external disruption.

Pineapple Financial states that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) within the five-calendar-day grace period allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25 for late quarterly reports. This means investors should anticipate the report to be submitted within a few days of the original due date, assuming no further complications arise.

Importantly for shareholders, the company does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the same quarter in the prior fiscal year, based on information available at the time of the notice. As with all such statements, this expectation is forward-looking and subject to change once the audit is fully completed and the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) is officially filed.

Pineapple Financial emphasizes that it is working to complete the necessary financial reporting and audit procedures as quickly as possible to remain in compliance with its SEC reporting obligations. The notification was signed on January 14, 2026, by Chief Financial Officer Sarfraz Habib, underscoring executive-level oversight and responsibility for the delayed filing and the company’s ongoing compliance efforts.

The most recent analyst rating on (PAPL) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pineapple Financial stock, see the PAPL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PAPL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PAPL is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial quality—ongoing losses and negative operating/free cash flow—despite rapid revenue growth. Technicals remain broadly bearish with the price below key longer-term moving averages and a negative MACD, while valuation provides limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend. A positive corporate governance/strategy event modestly offsets these risks.

To see Spark’s full report on PAPL stock, click here.

More about Pineapple Financial

Average Trading Volume: 564,034

Current Market Cap: $1.67M

data.title: Norris Industries Delays Quarterly SEC Filing
data.description:
Norris Industries ( (NRIS) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Norris Industries, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying regulators and investors that it will be late in submitting its Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the quarter ended November 30, 2025. The delayed filing concerns its regular quarterly financial report, not an annual Form 10-K (Yearly Report) or Form 20-F (Yearly Report).

The company attributes the delay mainly to additional work needed to complete its financial closing process. In particular, management is still evaluating how to properly classify its Series A convertible preferred stock on the balance sheet. This technical accounting assessment has slowed down the completion of the overall quarterly reporting package and would require unreasonable effort or expense to finish by the original deadline.

Norris Industries states that it expects to file the outstanding Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) within the standard extension window allowed under SEC rules—specifically, within five calendar days after the original due date of the report.

From a financial impact standpoint, the company does not anticipate any significant change in its operating results compared with the same quarter in the prior year. In other words, despite the delay, management is not warning of major surprises in earnings. That said, all such statements about expected timing and the nature of results are forward-looking and depend on the completion of the remaining accounting and review work.

The company indicates that all other required SEC reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, suggesting that this is a one-off timing issue rather than a pattern of noncompliance. The notification is signed on behalf of Norris Industries, Inc. by CEO Patrick Norris, underscoring management’s responsibility for both the disclosure and the ongoing effort to finalize the quarterly financial statements and remain in compliance with reporting obligations.

More about Norris Industries

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: FirstSun to Acquire First Foundation in All-Stock Merger to Form $17 Billion Southwest Bank
data.description:
New activity is brewing for First Foundation ( (FFWM) ). The company has submitted a Form DEFM14A to the SEC, indicating an upcoming shareholder vote. This form gives shareholders all the information they need to develop an informed vote. Read on to learn more about the proposed company action.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On October 27, 2025, FirstSun Capital Bancorp and First Foundation Inc. entered into a definitive merger agreement under which First Foundation will merge into FirstSun in an all‑stock transaction, followed by the merger of First Foundation Bank into FirstSun’s Sunflower Bank, creating a combined Southwest-focused bank with approximately $17 billion in assets. First Foundation stockholders will receive 0.16083 shares of FirstSun common stock for each First Foundation common or equivalent preferred share (with cash in lieu of fractional shares), while all outstanding First Foundation Series C warrant holders will have their warrants automatically exercised on a cashless basis and settled partly in equity and a fixed $17.5 million cash pool before being converted into FirstSun shares; the deal is structured as a tax‑advantaged reorganization and is paired with a post‑closing balance sheet repositioning plan aimed at improving capital, credit quality and liquidity for the enlarged institution, which will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol FSUN.

The most recent analyst rating on (FFWM) stock is a Hold with a $6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on First Foundation stock, see the FFWM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FFWM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FFWM is a Neutral.

First Foundation’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its poor financial performance, with significant challenges in revenue, profitability, and leverage. While technical indicators show some bullish momentum, the valuation remains unattractive due to negative earnings. The recent executive appointments are a positive step but do not significantly offset the financial challenges.

To see Spark’s full report on FFWM stock, click here.

data.title: Stimcell Energetics Delays Quarterly SEC Filing
data.description:
Stimcell Energetics ( (STME) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stimcell Energetics Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the period ended November 30, 2025. The company states that it was unable to complete the financial and business information required for the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) by the normal SEC deadline without incurring unreasonable effort or expense. No specific internal control issues, auditor changes, or special committee reviews are cited; the explanation is framed as a timing and workload challenge in finalizing the quarterly numbers.

The company indicates that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) within five calendar days of the prescribed due date, which aligns with the standard grace period available under SEC rules for late quarterly filings. Stimcell Energetics also confirms that all other periodic reports required in the last 12 months have been filed on time, suggesting this is an isolated delay rather than part of a broader compliance problem.

Management does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the same quarter of the prior fiscal year, based on the earnings statements that will be included in the upcoming Form 10-Q (Quarter Report). While this statement is, by nature, forward-looking and subject to final accounting adjustments, the company is signaling to investors that it does not expect a major earnings surprise related to this delay.

Stimcell Energetics emphasizes that it is working to complete the remaining financial and business disclosures needed for the quarter and intends to remain in compliance with SEC reporting rules by using the allowed extension period. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer David Jeffs, dated January 15, 2026, underscoring executive-level responsibility for the filing and the company’s stated plan to bring the quarterly reporting current within the short extension window.

More about Stimcell Energetics

Stimcell Energetics Inc. is a Vancouver, British Columbia-based company. The filing does not provide business-line detail, but the name suggests a focus on energy or life-sciences-related technologies. The company is publicly reporting under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and maintains regular quarterly and annual SEC reporting obligations.
